details.branch_omission_reason: Tier 1 read-only evaluation; only operational ledger/session records are created.

next_action: run full document and audit review

summary: Completed full harness document design review

validation: node scripts/check-completion-gates.mjs initially FAIL because active TASK-20260426-059 ledger files made the worktree dirty while CURRENT_STATE still said no active task
strengths: Tier system, agent responsibilities, direct-communication exceptions, evidence_type-based Validator FAIL rules, Resource Failure handling, report/quality gates, and git handoff rules are coherent across authority documents.
Authority document headers match CURRENT_STATE and the task ledgers parse without warning.
Security rules cover prompt injection, secrets, MCP server risk, and Validator OWASP review expectations.

findings.summary: Completion gate cannot distinguish legal active-task ledger/session changes from leftover dirty work after the previous completed task unless CURRENT_STATE is updated at task start or the script reads active ledgers.

Task Spec SSOT is documented as mandatory, but strict-era ledgers TASK-20260426-038 and TASK-20260426-042 through 053 lack tasks/specs/*.json or TASK_CREATED.details.spec/spec_path, and the audit scripts do not enforce this for future tasks.
Quality-score recent-average policy depends on logs/quality-scores.jsonl order, but backfilled rows can be appended out of task/time order and distort the 'recent 5' trigger.
Header audit covers CURRENT_STATE authority rows only; auxiliary guides, README, CLAUDE.md, and product plan docs are outside the integrity gate even though operators may rely on them.
Manual scripts are strong but not wired into a pre-commit/CI gate, so operational rules still rely on agents remembering to run them.
AGENTS.md says mandatory docs are read before work starts, while the new-request rule says TASK_CREATED is first; the intended ordering should be worded more explicitly for non-reentry sessions.

recommended_next_actions: Patch completion gate and/or lifecycle docs so active Task state is recognized without requiring CURRENT_STATE to remain stale or be manually updated in every read-only task.
Add spec-presence validation for new strict-era tasks and append CORRECTION metadata for legacy missing-spec tasks if they should remain accepted.
Make quality-score trend calculations sort by recorded_at or task_id and add a small audit for out-of-order backfills.
Create a doc-class manifest that labels authority docs, operational guides, product docs, archive/plans, and which gates apply to each.
Run the three audit scripts from a single npm script or pre-commit/CI check before guide-maintenance commits.

summary: Reported harness document design evaluation and improvement priorities

details.branch_omission_reason: Tier 1 read-only evaluation; only operational ledger/session/spec records were created.
details.quality_score_skipped_reason: Tier 1 read-only evaluation; no guide change or formal report requested.
